decision: Approve no-short optimum as the default client portfolio.
reason: The no-short portfolio is operationally feasible for a mass-market robo-adviser and avoids borrowing, leverage, and suitability complexity.

decision: Keep bounded-short optimum for analytical comparison only.
reason: The bounded-short solution can improve expected utility, but it is not appropriate as the default retail recommendation because it depends on leverage and short implementation.
10 fund entries are still marked as proxy instead of final FSMOne funds.
The improvement is measurable but not large enough to justify making a leveraged solution the primary recommendation.
